perfectRound: Perfect round. Every clock was right on time!
hintLevelMessages: The short hand is pointing near the {hour}
The short hand is near {hour}. Is the long hand pointing up (:00) or down (:30)?
The long hand is near the number {nearestFive}. Each number means 5 minutes.
The long hand is past {nearestFive} ({nearestFiveMinutes} min). Count the extra ticks!
Remember: {hour} on the short hand. The long hand is near where {nearestFive} would be.
The short hand is about {hour} hours around. Count ticks from the top for minutes.
resetScoresConfirm: Are you sure? This will erase all your high scores.

levels.name: Hours Only
levels.description: Read the hour hand on a friendly clock.
levels.learningGoal: Understanding the "Little Hand" and its position.
tips: Look at the short hand only
The short hand points to the hour
Ignore the long hand for now

levels.name: The Half-Hour
levels.description: Learn what happens when the big hand points down.
levels.learningGoal: Introduction of the "Big Hand" and its 180-degree flip.
tips: The long hand pointing down means :30
First read the short hand for the hour

levels.name: Five-Minute Jumps
levels.description: Count by fives around the clock.
tips: Each number on the clock means 5 minutes

levels.name: The Minute Tracker
levels.description: Read any minute on the clock.
tips: Count the small ticks between the numbers
First find the nearest big number, then count ticks

levels.name: Standard Clock
levels.description: A real clock - no helper labels.
levels.learningGoal: Removing the 5-minute helper labels; relying on memory.
tips: No more helper labels - you can do it!
Use what you learned in earlier levels

levels.name: The Expert
levels.description: No numbers at all - just the hands and ticks.
levels.learningGoal: Removing all numbers; relying on spatial orientation only.
tips: No numbers at all - trust your instincts!
